DNAlluminati.jpg: The DNAlluminati, a blue light trans-illuminator I custom built. This is used to visualise DNA without using UV, which can reduce cloning efficiency by damaging DNA
3_Klebs_streak.jpg: Streak plate of two clinical strains of Klebsiella pneumoniae. I used these in a large-scale analysis of K. pneumoniae metabolism. For article: https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.3003559
anaerobic_growth.jpg: Anaerobic jar used to grow anaerobic bacteria. A candle is used to deplete oxygen. I used to work a lot with Lactabacillus and Clostridial species
antibiotics.jpg: Antibiotic concentration reference sheet I used during my PhD at CSIRO
8_bacteriocins.jpg: Inhibition of a Lactoplantibacillus isolate to mutants from the plantacyclin bacteriocin. For article: https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-020-74332-1
bin_roster.jpg: A plasmid map we made to assign a people to a bin roster at the Department of Microbiology, Monash University
bradford.jpg: A Bradford protein assay I ran to do a semi-quantitative analysis of my purified protein, as we did not have an antibody. For article: https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-020-74332-1
cell_counts.jpg: Cell counting I did after two-strain co-cultures to study K. pneumoniae metabolism. Performed at The Department of Infectious Diseases, Monash University. For article: https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.3003559
2_cloning.jpg: A DNA gel on a UV-transilluminator prior to gel purification and cloning, performed at RMIT University. For article: https://doi.org/10.1128/AEM.00392-21
drop_plate.jpg: The spot plate method to quantify CFU/mL. Performed at The Department of Infectious Diseases, Monash University. For article: https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.3003559
9_ethanol_bath.jpg: Ethanol + dry ice (ethanol bath) to snap-freeze electrocompetent cells I made during my PhD at CSIRO. For article: https://doi.org/10.1128/AEM.00392-21
5_flame.jpg: A cool pic of a bunsen burner flame. Lights out in the lab for this snap
freezer.jpg: A look into the freezers at The Department of Infectious Diseases, Monash University
gram_stain1.jpg: Light microscopy after a Gram stain. Cannot remember the organism
gram_stain2.jpg: Light microscopy after a Gram stain. Cannot remember the organism
growth_inhibition.jpg: A growth inhibition assay of Plantacyclin against indicator strains to quantify growth inhibition. For article: https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-020-74332-1
foray_into_bioinfo.jpg: Very early foray into bioinformatics and the command line during my PhD. Performed at RMIT University
4_la3.jpg: Ligalactobacillus agilis La3 transformed with recombinant vectors I constructed, allowing expression of fluorescent protein CreiLOV. Performed at RMIT University for article: https://doi.org/10.1128/AEM.00392-21
lab_bench_CSIRO.jpg: My first lab bench at CSIRO!
lab_bench_RMIT.jpg: My third lab bench at RMIT University!
lab_books.jpg: My lab books from my first postdoc at Griffith University
machine_learning.png: Machine learning code in my work on predicting outcomes for patients infected with Klebsiella pneumoniae
media.jpg: Media making guide for bacterial growth media, at the Department of Microbiology, Monash University. During my PhD.
6_multi_level_network.png: A multi-level network I made in Plotly to visualise the relationship between carbapenemase genes, plasmids and species. During my postdoctoral position at the Department of Infectious Diseases, Monash University
networks.png: Network layouts I was playing with, to fill any shape.
7_obsidian.png: My electronic lab notebook, using Obsidian. This is me downloading many genomes for outcomes for patients infected with Klebsiella pneumoniae.
patch_plate.jpg: A patch plate I am taking Ligalactobacillus agilis La3 transformants from, so I can bead-beat them in a 96-well plate to perform PCR. For article: https://doi.org/10.1128/AEM.00392-21
patch_plates.jpg: Patch plates of transformed Ligalactobacillus agilis La3, confirming successful double-crossover chromosomal mutagenesis via positive and negative resistance profiles. For article: https://doi.org/10.1128/AEM.00392-21
phase_sep.jpg: Addition of butanol to Lactoplantibacillus plantarum supernatant in step 1 of plantacyclin purification. Dropping funnels made my life easier. Performed at the Griffith Institute of Drug Discovery, Griffith University. For article: https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-020-74332-1
1_promoters.jpg: Expression of fluorescent protein CreiLOV in E. coli under five novel different Ligalactobacillus agilis La3 promoters I identified and constructed during my PhD. Performed at RMIT University for article: https://doi.org/10.1128/AEM.00392-21
server.png: How I access the MASSIVE high performance computing cluster visually, with Filezilla software
10_snv.png: Running pairwise Single Nucleotide Variant analysis on a dataset of ~106k carbapenemase-positive genomes. Performed at the Department of Infectious Diseases, Monash University. Run using my tool: https://github.com/bananabenana/time_weighted_SNV_clusterer
transwells.jpg: Transwells with co-cultures of various lineages of Klebsiella pneumoniae at the Department of Infectious Diseases, Monash University. For article: https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.3003559
western.jpg: A Western blot confirming the expression of recombinant NetB from the chromosome of Ligalactobacillus agilis La3 after 100 generations (n=3 colonies per passaging culture). For article: https://doi.org/10.1128/AEM.00392-21
western_OG.jpg: The first time I got a successful Western blot recombinant NetB from the chromosome of Ligalactobacillus agilis La3.
